articles.title: Aryna Sabalenka Wins Tennis Match in Miami
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka is a top tennis player who won her match in the Miami Open. She beat Caty McNally with scores of 6-4 and 6-2. Sabalenka is trying to win both the Indian Wells and Miami tournaments this year, which is called the 'Sunshine Double'. She has won eight matches in a row. Next, she will play against Zheng Qinwen from China. Other players like Elena Rybakina and Jessica Pegula also won their matches. The article talks about who is winning in the Miami Open tennis tournament.

articles.title: BTS Concert Surprise Affects Company's Money
articles.summary: BTS, a famous K-pop band, had a big free concert in Seoul, South Korea. All seven members performed together for the first time since 2022. Fewer fans came than expected—only about 104,000 instead of 250,000. This made shares in Hybe, the company that manages BTS, drop by 15.5%. The concert was also streamed on Netflix to many countries, which might have affected how many people attended in person. BTS released a new album called Arirang, which sold nearly 4 million copies on its first day. Hybe relies on BTS for money, and the band faces competition from other groups. The concert started their world tour with 82 dates.

articles.title: Scientists Twist a Superconductor and Find a Surprise
articles.summary: Scientists studied a special material called strontium ruthenate that can conduct electricity without losing energy, which is called a superconductor. This material has been a mystery since 1994 because it works differently from others. To learn more, researchers from Kyoto University twisted tiny pieces of the material in a lab. They thought twisting it would change how it becomes superconducting, but they found almost no change. This surprising result helps rule out some old ideas and points to new possibilities. It is a big step in solving a long-standing puzzle in physics and could help study other materials too.

articles.title: Floating Time Crystals That Break a Science Rule
articles.summary: Scientists have made a special kind of matter called a time crystal. It is made of tiny beads that float in the air using sound waves. These beads move back and forth in a steady pattern, like ticking. In this new time crystal, the beads do not follow a famous science rule by Isaac Newton. The rule says that when two things push each other, they push back equally. But here, bigger beads push smaller beads more than the smaller ones push back. This makes their movement uneven. The experiment was done by scientists at New York University. They used a small device that you can hold in your hand. Time crystals might help in future technologies like computers. They could also teach us about how our bodies keep time, like when we sleep and wake up.

articles.title: A Big Tennis Upset: Korda Beats Alcaraz
articles.summary: In a surprising tennis match at the Miami Open, Sebastian Korda beat Carlos Alcaraz, who is the world's number one player. Alcaraz started poorly in the first set, but he fought back to win the second set. In the third set, Korda stayed calm and broke Alcaraz's serve to take the lead. He then won the match on his second match point. After the game, Alcaraz said he is still improving and feels good about his progress, even though he lost. He mentioned that lower-ranked players like Korda can play without pressure against him. Korda, ranked 36th, will now play another match in the tournament. This was a big win for Korda because Alcaraz is a top player who won the Australian Open earlier this year.

articles.title: A Magical Tree and Screen Time
articles.summary: This article talks about a new movie based on a book called The Magic Faraway Tree. In the movie, a family moves to the countryside and finds a magical tree with fun characters. The actors in the movie, like Andrew Garfield and Claire Foy, share their thoughts about how screens and technology affect families. They say that tech companies use our attention, which is like a valuable thing, and this can make people feel disconnected from each other and nature. The actors think it's important for families to spend less time on devices and more time together in the real world. They also mention that parents and kids face challenges with technology today, and everyone should try to do something about it.
